news.t: Emerging Markets Bonds Still Look Good Compared to Treasuries
VanEck Debuts EMBX, Bringing Active Emerging Markets Bond Strategy with ETF Efficiency to Investors

overview.description: The VanEck Emerging Markets Bond ETF (EMBX) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in broad credit fixed income. The fund is an actively managed ETF that invests in a broad range of debt securities issued by governments, quasi-government entities or corporations located in emerging market countries. EMBX was launched on Oct 6, 2025 and is issued by VanEck.

data.title: Emerging Markets Bonds Still Look Good Compared to Treasuries
data.text: Emerging markets bonds and the related ETFs are delivering for investors. Meanwhile, other, supposedly more dependable, less risky corners of the bond market are dithering.

data.title: VanEck Debuts EMBX, Bringing Active Emerging Markets Bond Strategy with ETF Efficiency to Investors
data.text: Now trading under the ticker symbol EMBX, investors gain access to VanEck's world-class active Emerging Markets Bond team and flagship strategy with the daily transparency, intraday liquidity and effi...
